methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The physical ERC20 token balance of the PoolManager contract must always be greater than or equal to the recorded availableLiquidity for that asset.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the physical balance falls below availableLiquidity, the protocol is insolvent and cannot honor legitimate user withdrawals or borrows, leading to a bank run and loss of funds.

invariants.description: The total scaled cross supply of an asset must exactly equal the sum of all individual users' scaled cross supplies.
invariants.attack_scenario: If total scaled supply is less than the sum of user balances, the protocol accounts for fewer liabilities than it actually owes. The last users to withdraw will be unable to retrieve their funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalScaledSupplyEqualsSumOfUsers(address asset) public view { uint256 sum; for(uint i=0; i<users.length; i++) { sum += assetData.userScaledCrossSupply[users[i]]; } assert(assetData.totalScaledCrossSupply == sum); }

invariants.description: The total scaled cross borrow of a group must exactly equal the sum of all individual users' scaled cross borrows in that group.
invariants.attack_scenario: If total scaled borrow is greater than the sum of user borrows, the protocol accounts for more debt than it can actually collect from users, leading to systemic bad debt and insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalScaledBorrowEqualsSumOfUsers(address asset, uint8 groupId) public view { uint256 sum; for(uint i=0; i<users.length; i++) { sum += groupData.userScaledCrossBorrow[users[i]]; } assert(groupData.totalScaledCrossBorrow == sum); }

invariants.description: The supply index of an asset must be monotonically non-decreasing over time.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can manipulate the interest rate model or state to decrease the supply index, they can steal accrued yield from lenders or slash their principal.
invariants.invariant_logic: assetData.supplyIndex(t2) >= assetData.supplyIndex(t1) for all t2 >= t1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_supplyIndexMonotonicity(address asset) public view { assert(assetData.supplyIndex >= previousSupplyIndex[asset]); }

invariants.description: The borrow index of a group must be monotonically non-decreasing over time.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can decrease the borrow index, they can arbitrarily forgive their own debt and the debt of others, leading to a massive loss of value for the protocol.
invariants.invariant_logic: groupData.borrowIndex(t2) >= groupData.borrowIndex(t1) for all t2 >= t1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_borrowIndexMonotonicity(address asset, uint8 groupId) public view { assert(groupData.borrowIndex >= previousBorrowIndex[asset][groupId]); }

invariants.description: The collateral factor of an asset must always be less than or equal to its liquidation threshold.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the collateral factor exceeds the liquidation threshold, users can borrow up to the collateral factor and instantly become undercollateralized. This allows attackers to extract maximum value and leave the protocol with bad debt.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_collateralFactorUnderLiquidationThreshold(address asset) public view { assert(assetData.collateralFactor <= assetData.liquidationThreshold); }

invariants.description: A user's health factor must be strictly greater than or equal to the liquidation threshold (1e18) immediately after executing a borrow operation.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user can borrow assets such that their health factor drops below 1.0, they are instantly liquidatable, allowing them to bypass collateralization requirements and saddle the protocol with bad debt.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_healthFactorPostBorrow(address user) public view { assert(calculateHealthFactor(user) >= 1e18); }

invariants.description: The global totalDebtShare in the yield staking module must exactly equal the sum of debtShare across all active and unstaking YieldStakeData entries.
invariants.attack_scenario: If totalDebtShare is artificially inflated or deflated, the conversion rate between debt shares and underlying debt assets is skewed. This could allow users to repay significantly less debt than they owe, draining the lending pool.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_yieldTotalDebtShareConservation() public view { uint256 sum; for(uint i=0; i<allStakes.length; i++) { sum += stakeDatas[allStakes[i].nft][allStakes[i].tokenId].debtShare; } assert(totalDebtShare == sum); }
